RECORD TABLE 
 
When the quality of the products changes, the QuID is updated and a row is added 
to this table.  The third column specifies which sections or sub-sections have been 
updated.  The fourth column should mention the version of the product to which the 
change applies.

4 Quality Information Document for OMI Ocean Heat Content 
© EU Copernicus Marine Service – Public   
CMEMS-MED-OMI-QUID-CLIMATE_OHC 
Issue 1.4 – June 2025 
1) INTRODUCTION 
a) Product Name  
 
OMI_CLIMATE_OHC_MEDSEA_area_averaged_anomalies 
 
b) Indicator Evaluation Method 
Ocean heat content is defined here as the deviation from a reference period (1993-
2014) and is closely proportional to the average temperature change from z1 = 0 m to 
z2 = 700 m depth: 
OHC = ∫ 𝜌0
z2
z1
cp(T𝑦𝑟 − Tclim)dz         [1] 
with a reference density of = 1030 kgm-3 and a specific heat capacity of  
cp = 3980 J kg-1 °C-1 (e.g. von Schuckmann et al., 2009). 
Time series of annual mean values area averaged ocean heat content is provided for 
the Mediterranean Sea (30°N, 46°N; 6°W, 36°E) and is evaluated in areas where the 
topography is deeper than 700m. 
 
 
 
 
5 Quality Information Document for OMI Ocean Heat Content 
© EU Copernicus Marine Service – Public   
CMEMS-MED-OMI-QUID-CLIMATE_OHC 
Issue 1.4 – June 2025

catalog_entry.summary: This Quality Information Document for OMI Ocean Heat Content provides details on the evaluation method, scientific validation, and input products used to compute ocean heat content anomalies in the Mediterranean Sea.

product_line.name: Ocean Heat Content Anomaly (0-700m) for the Mediterranean Sea
